title: PSA Purification and Tail-Gas Integration
caption: Expected PSA cascade recovery and purity output.
description: The PSA cells should show cascade recovery improving with bed count while purity remains high. Tail-gas flow is intentionally retained as a fuel-balance output rather than hidden in a single recovery number.
discussion: The parameter study shows how hydrogen purification after shifted syngas responds when the controlling parameter changes. The important result is not a single base-case value; it is the shape of the response and the point where H2 purity, recovery, tail-gas composition, cycle assumptions, and CAPEX begin to trade against margin or cost.
calculation_basis: Stoichiometric SMR/ATR/POX/WGS balances, equilibrium or route-builder sweeps, PSA recovery accounting, and CO2-capture mass-balance indices.
study_basis: IEA hydrogen route context plus process-design and gas-purification references set the screening ranges and interpretation limits.
source_labels: IEA Global Hydrogen Review 2023, Chemical engineering design reference, Gas purification reference, GPSA engineering data reference

topic_examples.calculation: Run a PSACascade bed-count and equalisation sweep on shifted syngas.
topic_examples.sweep: Number of beds, equalisation steps, recovery target, and tail-gas return assumption.
topic_examples.kpis: H2 purity, H2 recovery, tail-gas heating value index, PSA CAPEX index.
topic_examples.source_labels: Gas purification reference, seader2016, GPSA engineering data reference
